channel.title: +Articles on आगमे ब्राह्मे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on आगमे ब्राह्मे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.title: Vaṇśaṭhakōpadayā
item.description:
Source: here.

Vaṇśaṭhakōpadayā

मङ्गलम्

पद्मानायक पाद-पद्म-युगले पर्याय-भृंगात्मना
चेतो मे चिर-पक्षपात-विभवाद् आस्वादम् आसीदतु ।
अध्यात्म-श्रुति-दुग्ध-सिन्धु-लहरी संचार-सम्भावितैः
हंस-श्रीर् अधिगम्यते सुकृतिभिर् यत्रोल्लसद्-बुद्धिभिः ॥

उद्गृह्णतीम् उपनिषत्सु निगूढमर्थं
चित्ते निवेशयितुम् अल्पधियां स्वयं नः ।
पश्येम लक्ष्मणमुनेः प्रतिपन्न-हस्तां
उन्निद्र-पद्म-सुभगाम् उपदेश-मुद्राम् ॥

Intro

Of late there has been a tendency among certain neo cults and modern Advaitic thinkers like here to quote from certain dubious Upaniṣads which they claim is a part of the “108” listed in the Muktikā canon. We attempt to analyse the legitimacy of such claims and draw a line between true Śrutis and bogus ones.

item.title: dAra-shikoH list
item.description:
Archive link

On Wed, May 1, 2013 at 8:22 AM, Omkar Patil wrote:

The first mention of the muktika upanishad in recorded literature is in 1656 in one of the works of Dara Shikoh, the son of Shah Jahan

No, that is not right. Dara Shikoh had collected 52 Upanishads in his ‘Sirr-i-Akbar’, and nowhere did he mention Muktika Upanishad. I had pulled this list a couple of years back, when a learned friend said that this is the first reference to sharabha upanishad (which isn’t the case either) -
